Furthermore, you receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. If you did not gain a place, you may be listed on the waiting list or be offered an alternative parallel course. If students deregister from a course/exam and a waiting list exists, you can move up the waiting list. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. In case of long waiting lists, the directorate of studies (SPL) will probably offer additional parallel courses, for which you may register. In this case, you will be able to change the number of points you allocated before a second allocation may start. If you gained an alternative place (remaining place) in a parallel course for which remaining places are available, you have to confirm or reject your place within the deadline defined by the directorate of studies (SPL). If you reject the place, you will be automatically deregistered from the course. The allocated points can not be transferred to the next semester. retrieved.title: Attending courses out of interest
heading_path: Your steps in the admission procedure
retrieved.text:
1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) withing the application period. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Log into u:space to complete the registration.
3. At "non-degree programme" you will find a link for the [Statistical Data Confirmation (UHStat1)](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/statistische-daten). Fill in the questionnaire and choose "Check and submit".

#### Registration in u:space

1. Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/2148) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
2. Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
3. Pay the [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/). The admission is completed as soon as the prescribed fee is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can order your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en).

Register for the courses you want to attend. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

retrieved.title: Making up for supplementary examinations from the bachelor programme for admission to a master programme
retrieved.text:
1. Register in u:space
   If this is your first time studying at the University of Vienna: Activate your u:account in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/) within the application period. Make sure you remember the login data, because you will further need the u:account during your studies. Log in to u:space, complete the registration.
2. Apply for admission to a non-degree programme
   Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/61/create/1644) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
3. Wait for an answer
   Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
4. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee.
   The admission is completed as soon as the [prescribed fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can validate your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) or order it if you do not yet have one.

Register for the required courses. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

retrieved.text: students deregister, you might be able to move up the list and get a free place. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. - Alternative place available (Alternativplatz verfügbar): You have not gained a place on the course of your interest, but will be able to attend another group (parallel course). You can accept or reject it within a defined deadline. - In process (In Bearbeitung): The registration period has expired and the allocation of places or your record is being processed. This can take several hours. In the meanwhile, you cannot make any changes. - Closed (geschlossen): At the end of the semester, all registrations are closed, which enables you to register for the same or a similar course or exam without any problems in the following semester. - **No place received** (Keinen Platz erhalten): You have been on the waiting list of a course/exam and did not receive a place. You can no longer move up the list. You can register for an equal course/exam in the following semester. retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: / - Re-enrolment In order to continue your studies at the University of Vienna, you must pay your tuition fee/Students' Union (ÖH) fee in due time. If you deliberately do not pay the fee (because you do not want to continue your studies) or if you forget to pay the fee, you lose your admission to studies. In case you have paid the fee at a different Austrian university, you have to re-enrol in your studies at the University of Vienna. You can do that until the end of the admission period. If your fourth attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires on the day of the exam. You can take exams that are not part of the STEOP four times. The last attempt takes place before an examination committee. If the last attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires. At the University of Vienna, re-admission to the same degree programme and other degree programmes that require you to complete the exam that you failes four times is not possible. retrieved.title: Additional bachelor/diploma programme
retrieved.text:
1. Complete the [Online-Self-Assessment](http://self-assessment.univie.ac.at/) (OSA) of the desired degree programme. Afterwards you receive your OSA code, which you will need in the further procedure.
2. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to study additionally within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Enter your OSA code.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
3. You can be admitted to the new degree programme if you fulfil all requirements. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address after a processing time of few weeks ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)). You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.
4. The admission does not ensue automatically. Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/933) after you have received your admission letter. Indicate which degree programme you would like to be admitted to. After a few working days, the new degree programme will be visible in u:space > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail.

With the point allocation system, you receive a certain number of points (= points account) every semester which you can use for the registration. You will have a points account for the registration for courses and another one for the registration for exams.

If you register for courses offered by different directorates of studies, you will have several points accounts. The points can be used for courses with a limited number of participants.

The more points you allocate to a course, the more likely it is that you are accepted.

The allocation of points can still be changed within the [registration deadline](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741417). You cannot save points for the following semester, which means that you will lose points that you have not used.

You can find further information in the [ABC of terminology.](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741407)

1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) withing the application period. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.

The admission is completed as soon as the prescribed fee is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal matters > My documents.

In the meanwhile, you cannot make any changes. - Closed (geschlossen): At the end of the semester, all registrations are closed, which enables you to register for the same or a similar course or exam without any problems in the following semester. - **No place received** (Keinen Platz erhalten): You have been on the waiting list of a course/exam and did not receive a place. You can no longer move up the list. You can register for an equal course/exam in the following semester. reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: / - Re-enrolment In order to continue your studies at the University of Vienna, you must pay your tuition fee/Students' Union (ÖH) fee in due time. If you deliberately do not pay the fee (because you do not want to continue your studies) or if you forget to pay the fee, you lose your admission to studies. In case you have paid the fee at a different Austrian university, you have to re-enrol in your studies at the University of Vienna. You can do that until the end of the admission period. Related Links: - [Re-enrolment](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) - [Payment of the tuition fee/Students’ Union (ÖH) fee](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) - [Tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/) Related entries: - [Students’ Union fee (ÖH fee)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31205&cHash=ed830f954f65ec47794ec4809327b5d1) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - [Continuation of studies](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31960&cHash=051426adf043f8ab058f4e6c4520bb53) - Re-sitting an exam It is possible to re-sit a STEOP exam three times. If your fourth attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires on the day of the exam. You can take exams that are not part of the STEOP four times. The last attempt takes place before an examination committee. If the last attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires. At the University of Vienna, re-admission to the same degree programme and other degree programmes that require you to complete the exam that you failes four times is not possible. Related entries: - [Board examination](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31160&cHash=97b4b803d01ac1fde81b560e8d2ab5fa)

reranked.title: Additional bachelor/diploma programme
reranked.text:
1. Complete the [Online-Self-Assessment](http://self-assessment.univie.ac.at/) (OSA) of the desired degree programme. Afterwards you receive your OSA code, which you will need in the further procedure.
2. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to study additionally within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Enter your OSA code.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
3. You can be admitted to the new degree programme if you fulfil all requirements. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address after a processing time of few weeks ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)). You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.
4. The admission does not ensue automatically. Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/933) after you have received your admission letter. Indicate which degree programme you would like to be admitted to. After a few working days, the new degree programme will be visible in u:space > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail.

reranked.title: Planning your semester
reranked.text: for the current semester and create your personal schedule. In the semester planner, you will find a calendar that shows all planned courses and exams for your degree programmes. This allows you to quickly find and resolve overlapping dates. Alternatively, you can also use the [record of examinations in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/pruefungspass) which outlines the structure of your curriculum. This way, you can see at a glance which achievements you have already completed and which ones you have to complete next. In the [course directory u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/de/index.html), you can select the specific courses for the current semester. Factors, such as content, time, venue, lecturer, etc., may help you in your decision. The workload required to complete a course is indicated in the form of ECTS credits. On average, completing a degree programme within its standard duration takes 30 ECTS credits per semester. Please note that you have to complete an [introductory and orientation period (STEOP)](/en/semester-planning/introductory-and-orientation-period-steop/) at the beginning of every bachelor’s programme before you are allowed to register for any other courses. In addition to compulsory and elective modules, you will have to complete [extension curricula](/en/extension-curricula-and-alternative-extensions/) (EC) in almost all bachelor’s programmes. You can register for EC in u:space. - [Waiting list and allocation of remaining places](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741429)

With the point allocation system, you receive a certain number of points (= points account) every semester which you can use for the registration. You will have a points account for the registration for courses and another one for the registration for exams.

If you register for courses offered by different directorates of studies, you will have several points accounts. The points can be used for courses with a limited number of participants.

The more points you allocate to a course, the more likely it is that you are accepted.

The allocation of points can still be changed within the [registration deadline](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741417). You cannot save points for the following semester, which means that you will lose points that you have not used.

You can find further information in the [ABC of terminology.](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741407)

selected.text: / - Waiting list and allocation of remaining places (courses) After the end of a registration deadline, you can check whether or not you gained a place on a lecture/an exam in u:space under Studies > Registration Overview. Furthermore, you receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. If you did not gain a place, you may be listed on the waiting list or be offered an alternative parallel course. If students deregister from a course/exam and a waiting list exists, you can move up the waiting list. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. In case of long waiting lists, the directorate of studies (SPL) will probably offer additional parallel courses, for which you may register. In this case, you will be able to change the number of points you allocated before a second allocation may start. If you gained an alternative place (remaining place) in a parallel course for which remaining places are available, you have to confirm or reject your place within the deadline defined by the directorate of studies (SPL). If you reject the place, you will be automatically deregistered from the course. The allocated points can not be transferred to the next semester. If you neither confirm nor reject the place, you remain on the waiting list. Related Links: - [Registration

heading_path: Your steps in the admission procedure
selected.text:
1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) withing the application period. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Log into u:space to complete the registration.
3. At "non-degree programme" you will find a link for the [Statistical Data Confirmation (UHStat1)](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/statistische-daten). Fill in the questionnaire and choose "Check and submit".

#### Registration in u:space

1. Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/2148) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
2. Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
3. Pay the [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/). The admission is completed as soon as the prescribed fee is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can order your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en).

Register for the courses you want to attend. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Sources:

Furthermore, you receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. If you did not gain a place, you may be listed on the waiting list or be offered an alternative parallel course. If students deregister from a course/exam and a waiting list exists, you can move up the waiting list. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. In case of long waiting lists, the directorate of studies (SPL) will probably offer additional parallel courses, for which you may register. In this case, you will be able to change the number of points you allocated before a second allocation may start. If you gained an alternative place (remaining place) in a parallel course for which remaining places are available, you have to confirm or reject your place within the deadline defined by the directorate of studies (SPL). If you reject the place, you will be automatically deregistered from the course. The allocated points can not be transferred to the next semester. 2. Log into u:space to complete the registration.
3. At "non-degree programme" you will find a link for the [Statistical Data Confirmation (UHStat1)](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/statistische-daten). Fill in the questionnaire and choose "Check and submit".

#### Registration in u:space

1. Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/2148) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
2. Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
3. Pay the [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/). The admission is completed as soon as the prescribed fee is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal matters > My documents.
